commit.message:
security: fix remaining audit findings A1, M11, B1-B3, B12

A1 — Seed script now raises RuntimeError in production if
     SEED_ADMIN_PASSWORD / SEED_MEMBER_PASSWORD env vars are not set,
     refusing to fall back to the known hardcoded default.

M11 — POST /api/invitations/accept now rate-limited (5 req / 15 min
      per client IP) to prevent token brute-force attacks.

B1-B3 — Fixed missing tildes: «Titulo» → «Título» in 7 files
        (tasks-page, client-detail-page, lead-detail-page,
        industry-news-page, proposals-page, inbox-note-card,
        evidence-list).

B12 — CORS_ORIGINS env var now validated at startup: raises
      RuntimeError if '*' is present in production to prevent
      credential-bearing cross-origin requests from any domain.

B11 confirmed already covered by Pydantic TaskStatus enum validation
(returns 422 on invalid values — no code change needed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- APP_TEAM_SPEC.md: base URL + Swift service URL → https://nutritionapi.uk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security hardening: 452-issue fix from HostileReview report b6d698032c26

Comprehensive security audit remediation covering 110 critical and 342 high
severity issues across 88 files. Key changes:

- Tenant isolation enforced across all adapters, engines, and query paths
- SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries and identifier validation
- Hardcoded credentials removed, replaced with env var references
- Path traversal protection on all file operations
- DEMO_MODE changed from hardcoded True to env-var controlled, default False
- Authentication enabled by default in all deployment configs
- Dependency versions pinned (no more >= or latest tags)
- PII scrubbing in logs, monitoring, and statistics
- Rate limiting with memory caps on all server endpoints
- CSP headers and IPC channel allowlists in Electron
- Wound-wait deadlock resolution replacing naive youngest-abort
- Query complexity limits (length, depth, token count, join count)
- Docker ports bound to 127.0.0.1, image digests pinned
- Certificate generation upgraded to 4096-bit RSA minimum
- Error messages sanitized to prevent information leakage

feat: deliver --json + recovery-aware errors (3.3.0) (#160)

- Add --json flag to `carson deliver` for machine-readable output.
  JSON envelope includes command, branch, pr_number, pr_url, ci,
  merged, exit_code, and on failure: error and recovery fields.

  "Recovery: <command>", JSON output includes a recovery field.

  for unified JSON/human output rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Normalize 16-bit label images to 8-bit for proper display

CZI and other formats store label images as 16-bit, causing dark/low-
contrast display when converted via getRGB(). Now normalizes from raw
raster data, mapping the actual value range to 0-255. Also detects
8-bit images with narrow dynamic range (e.g., unusual color models)
and normalizes those as well. Applied at retrieval time so all callers
(single OCR, batch, and scripting) benefit equally.

commit.message:
perf: fix AGS progressive slowdown by replacing polling with signals

Replace continuous GLib.timeout_add polling loops with GObject notify::
signal connections in bar widgets, and gate QuickSettings window timers
behind visibility so they only run while the panel is open.

- QuickSettings window: start/stop 4 polling timers on window-toggled
  (stops WiFi widget churn and nmcli subprocess spawning in background)
- Volume, Microphone: notify::volume/mute signals on WirePlumber endpoint
- Network: notify::ssid/strength/internet/enabled on AstalNetwork
- Battery: notify::percentage/charging/is-present on AstalBattery
- QuickSettings bar button: signals instead of 1000ms poll

Also adds docs/software/ with ags-performance-degradation.md documenting
root cause and solution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Farcaster miniapp integration with dual auth

- Install @farcaster/miniapp-sdk and @farcaster/quick-auth
- Add /.well-known/farcaster.json manifest (homeUrl/iconUrl set to streethunt.vercel.app)
- Detect FC miniapp context on init (sdk.context); falls back to existing email OTP for web
- New handleFarcasterAuth: reads FC profile (fid, username, pfpUrl), registers user via /api/fc-auth, loads from localStorage cache on return visits
- New pages/api/fc-auth.js: verifies Quick Auth JWT, upserts user in Supabase via service key
- pages/api/validate.js: dual auth — FC Quick Auth JWT or Supabase JWT both accepted
- validateSticker uses sdk.quickAuth.fetch in FC context (auto-attaches Bearer token)
- IS_FARCASTER flag skips direct Supabase client calls for FC users (localStorage-backed)
- sdk.actions.ready() called at end of every init path to dismiss splash screen
- Domain derived from NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL (no new env var needed)

commit.message:
Add sound mute toggle (#146) (#149)

* Add mute state and persistence to audio.js (#146)

- Add module-level muted flag with localStorage persistence
- Export setMuted, isMuted, toggleMute, loadMutePreference
- Gate all play functions: early-return when muted
- Add comprehensive tests for mute behavior and edge cases

* Draw speaker icon in drawHUD (#146)

- Add muted parameter to drawHUD signature (backward-compatible default)
- Draw speaker cone + wave bars when unmuted (PALETTE.pole)
- Draw speaker cone + diagonal slash when muted (PALETTE.enemyBody)
- Add tests for mute icon rendering differences

* Wire M key mute toggle in game.js (#146)

- Import isMuted, toggleMute, loadMutePreference from audio.js
- Load mute preference on construction
- Add debounced M key listener (same pattern as pause key)
- Pass isMuted() to both drawHUD call sites
- Add KeyM tracking tests to input.test.js

* Add touch mute button (#146)

- Add mute button to TouchControls with onMuteToggle callback
- Position at top-right with 4px touch padding
- Draw rounded rect with scaled speaker icon inside
- Rising-edge detection prevents repeated toggles
- Pass toggleMute callback from game.js
- Update touch tests for mute button

* Update audio mocks with new mute exports (#146)

Add isMuted, toggleMute, setMuted, loadMutePreference to audio.js
mocks in game, game-flow, game-integration, and pause-resume tests.

* Document M key mute toggle in README (#146)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: full speech pipeline — nimcp_brain_speak() + Python binding + WebSocket server

Wire Broca/Wernicke/lexical infrastructure into a working language production
pipeline. Athena can now generate spoken text from neural state.

Layer 1 — C Core:
- Rewrite lpb_semantic_to_tokens() to use real Wernicke lexicon (cosine
  similarity + frequency weighting), with heuristic fallback
- Add lpb_connect_lexical_access() to connect lexicon to production bridge
- Wire language_orchestrator_generate_output() — lexical selection from
  Wernicke → Broca motor planning → text concatenation (was a stub)
- Add nimcp_brain_speak() public API
- Seed lexicon with ~50 common English words during Wernicke init
- Fix pre-existing wernicke_immune_bridge type mismatch warnings

Layer 2 — Python:
- Add Brain.speak(semantic_vector) → dict with text, confidence, fluency
- Add LanguageProducer class for autoregressive multi-token generation
- Add Parent.teach_speech() — "repeat after me" exercises at each stage
- Integrate speech training into developmental stages 1/2/3

Layer 3 — Client:
- New athena_server.py: FastAPI + WebSocket chat/voice server
- TTSEngine (piper/espeak), SoundEngine (cat, dog, bird, music, etc.)
- Browser chat UI with microphone support and audio playback

Also: clean up ~150 obsolete one-off fix/migration scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🔧 Phase-2: update hook paths from octiv to current project paths

Optimize full pipeline: perf, reliability, prompts, and docs.

- helpers.py: @lru_cache for GCS client; extract _build_litellm_body + _is_retryable_litellm_status to eliminate ~80 lines of duplication
- veo_api.py: thread-local Vertex AI Session (TLS reuse); _poll_video_ready initial_wait=20s; async chain jobs with TTL eviction + hard cap; shared executor
- audit.py: async queue + daemon writer thread for non-blocking file persistence
- shoplive_api.py: LLM response TTL cache (5min) with api_base hash in key; _base() helper for workflow responses
- briefing.py: O(n) dedup for selling points; aspect_ratio silent-override tracking
- infra.py: use shared ThreadPoolExecutor instead of per-attempt pool creation
- web_app.py: module-level OpenAPI spec cache
- tests: add test_optimizations.py (19 tests) and update test_audit.py for async writes
- docs: add architecture.md (full system chain) and prompts.md (all LLM prompts + templates)

tap the field they want, and the scrollIntoView handler ensures
it stays visible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: Issue #50 spec — Core/Reference bypass of two-signal architecture

Documents the architectural gap between Core/Reference identity gates and
the two-signal signal framework. Key findings:

- Core cannot be a scholarship category: auteur identity is Signal 1-only;
  no structural coordinates exist for Signal 2 to fire independently.
  Core stays as an identity gate (Option A).

- Reference CAN be a scholarship category: the 50-film canon is modeled
  on published canons (Sight & Sound, AFI, Ebert) equivalent in authority
  to Satellite scholarship sources. Migrate to data/corpora/reference_canon.csv
  following Issue #38 corpus pattern — Reference films get reason=corpus_lookup,
  appear in accuracy baseline.

- Current gap: ~200 Core + 50 Reference films are outside the signal
  measurement framework; scholarship_only contract was built to work around
  this. Reference migration closes the gap for ~50 films at low risk.

Recommended path: Reference corpus migration (separate implementation issue).
Defer Option B (post-signal Core routing) until Reference migration is stable.

commit.message:
Fix Supabase SDK CDN failure breaking auth for returning users

The Supabase JS SDK loaded from jsdelivr intermittently fails with
net::ERR_FAILED, causing a ReferenceError on createClient() that
cascades into auth failure — returning users get stuck on the loading
screen. The service worker compounded this by swallowing script fetch
errors (returning undefined instead of re-throwing).

- Pin SDK to @2.98.0 with explicit UMD path, add unpkg fallback CDN
- Fix sw.js catch handler to re-throw on non-document requests so
  <script onerror> actually fires
- Guard supabase-client.js initialize() against missing SDK global
- Add retry logic in ui-auth.js: wait 2.5s for fallback, retry once
- Show user-facing "Connection unavailable" instead of dev error strings
- Add 9 unit tests covering SDK missing, retry, and error messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
